title: linaria odora
intro: Linaria odora, also known as sweet-eyed toadflax, is a flowering plant in the figwort family that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Linaria odora include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Linaria odora is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
